parameters.name: sourcegrp
parameters.label: Source Group
parameters.description: Limit the particle emission to the points found in the specified point groups.

parameters.name: prtype
parameters.label: Particle Type
parameters.description: Selects how the particles are rendered.

parameters.name: behave
parameters.label: Behavior
parameters.description: Select between emitting particles from the geometry's points or deforming the original geometry using the Particle 's behavior.

parameters.name: normals
parameters.label: Compute Normals
parameters.description: Creates normals on the geometry.  Only used when is set to .

parameters.name: ptreuse
parameters.label: Point Reuse
parameters.description: Decide how the internal memory for points is reused when a point needs to be created or when a point dies.

parameters.name: timepreroll
parameters.label: Preroll Time
parameters.description: How many seconds of the simulation to bypass, after the reset time is reached. For example, if you put the number into this field (and reset is at ), frame one will show the simulation that was at a t

parameters.name: timeinc
parameters.label: Time Inc
parameters.description: The parameter determines how often to cook the . By default, this parameter is set to . This means that the will cook once for every frame. When complex dynamics are involved, the may require more fre

parameters.name: maxsteps
parameters.label: Max Steps
parameters.description: Limits how far back in time TouchDesigner calculates particle positions for proper interactions.  If frame rates are slow this computation back in time can become high, this parameter limits that effe

parameters.name: jitter
parameters.label: Jitter Births
parameters.description: This allows you to jitter the location pixels of each particle as they are born.

parameters.name: accurate
parameters.label: Accurate Moves
parameters.description: This option makes the particles move more accurately between frames by calculating their trajectories for fractional frame values.

parameters.name: rmunused
parameters.label: Remove Unused Points
parameters.description: Removes all unused points from the input geometry. This is provided as an explicit option instead of automatically because it saves the time needed to purge the points from memory during the simulatio

parameters.name: attractmode
parameters.label: Attractor Use
parameters.description: Select which mode of attraction to use for Surface Attractors.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: When On the particle system is held in a reset state and does not emit particles.

parameters.name: resetpulse
parameters.label: Reset Pulse
parameters.description: Instantly reset the particle system to its starting state.

parameters.name: period
parameters.label: Turb Period
parameters.description: A small period means that the turbulence varies quickly over a small area, while a larger value will cause points close to each other to be affected similarly.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description: Random number seed for the particle simulation.

parameters.name: doid
parameters.label: Add Particle ID
parameters.description: Adds an ID number to each particle as it is born. New attributes only appear once the particle system is reset via the parameter or loads for first time.

parameters.name: domass
parameters.label: Add Mass Attribute
parameters.description: When selected, calculates the mass of the particle, as specified in the field.

parameters.name: mass
parameters.label: Mass
parameters.description: The relative mass of each particle. Heavier particles take longer to start moving, and longer to slow down.

parameters.name: dodrag
parameters.label: Add Drag Attribute
parameters.description: When selected, calculates the drag coefficient of the particle, as entered in the field.

parameters.name: drag
parameters.label: Drag
parameters.description: Drag of each particle.

parameters.name: birth
parameters.label: Birth
parameters.description: The number of particles born each second. Particles are not released in clusters. They are born at random times during the first frame of their existence. Their birth time is set randomly during the f

parameters.name: life
parameters.label: Life Expect
parameters.description: How long each particle will exist, in seconds. The default is seconds. You may want to adjust this number based on the length of your animation.

parameters.name: lifevar
parameters.label: Life Variance
parameters.description: Variance of a particle's life expectancy in seconds. If life expectancy is one second, and the variance is zero seconds, each particle will live exactly one second. If variance is set to 0.5, then som

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.label: Alpha Speed
parameters.description: As a particle goes faster, it should become more transparent. The parameter defaults to 0, which doesn't change alpha as the speed increases. A typical value of 0.5 causes the particle to be 70% opaqu

parameters.name: birthcount
parameters.label: Birth Count
parameters.description: Specifies the number of particles created when the Birth pulse parameter is hit.

parameters.name: birthpulse
parameters.label: Birth
parameters.description: Manually create n particles when pulsed. The number of particles created is controlled by the Birth Count parameter.

parameters.name: hit
parameters.label: Hit Behavior
parameters.description: Control over what happens when a particle hits either the six collision planes or the collide object. The options are:

parameters.name: gaintan
parameters.label: Gain Tangent
parameters.description: Friction parameters which can be regarded as energy loss upon collision. The first parameter affects the energy loss (gain) perpendicular to the surface. 0 means all energy (velocity) is lost, 1 means

parameters.name: gainnorm
parameters.label: Gain Normal
parameters.description: Friction parameters which can be regarded as energy loss upon collision. The first parameter affects the energy loss (gain) perpendicular to the surface. 0 means all energy (velocity) is lost, 1 means

parameters.name: splittype
parameters.label: Split
parameters.description: Select if the particle will split and under what conditions.

parameters.name: surfattract
parameters.label: Surfattract
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: externalz
parameters.label: Externalz
parameters.description: Forces of gravity acting on the particles. When drag is zero, the particles can accelerate with no limit on their speed.

parameters.name: splitmax
parameters.label: Splitmax
parameters.description: When a particle splits, it splits into a number of other particles. The number of particles is randomly set between this range.

parameters.name: windz
parameters.label: Windz
parameters.description: Wind forces acting on the particles. Similar to External Force. Using Wind (and no other forces, such as Turbulence), the particles will not exceed the wind velocity.  ====Discussion - Wind vs Externa

parameters.name: splitvarz
parameters.label: Splitvarz
parameters.description: This is a random amount that is added to the split velocity. When creating fireworks, the variance is large while the velocity is low. When rendering raindrops splashing, the split velocity is large i

parameters.name: limitnegz
parameters.label: Limitnegz
parameters.description: The particles will die or bounce off the limit planes when it reaches them. The six limit plane fields define a bounding cube. The default settings are 1000 units away, which is very large. Reduce the

parameters.name: splitvelz
parameters.label: Splitvelz
parameters.description: Each split particle is given this base velocity.

parameters.name: turbz
parameters.label: Turbz
parameters.description: The amplitude of turbulent (chaotic) forces along each axis. Use positive values, if any.

parameters.name: limitposz
parameters.label: Limitposz
parameters.description: The particles will die or bounce off the limit planes when it reaches them. The six limit plane fields define a bounding cube. The default settings are 1000 units away, which is very large. Reduce the
